This paper builds upon the observation that max-min fairness guarantees these properties under a strong assumption---user demands being static over time---and that, for the realistic case of dynamic user demands, max-min fairness loses one or more of these properties. We present Karma, a generalization of max-min fairness for dynamic user demands. The key insight in Karma is to introduce "memory" into max-min fairness --- when allocating resources, Karma takes users' past allocations into account: in each quantum, users donate their unused resources and are assigned credits when other users borrow these resources; Karma carefully orchestrates exchange of credits across users (based on their instantaneous demands, donated resources and borrowed resources), and performs prioritized resource allocation based on users' credits. We prove theoretically that Karma guarantees Pareto efficiency, online strategy-proofness, and optimal fairness for dynamic user demands (without future knowledge of user demands). Empirical evaluations over production workloads show that these properties translate well into practice: Karma is able to reduce disparity in performance across users to a bare minimum while maintaining Pareto-optimal system-wide performance.
items.abstract_zh: 用于资源分配的经典最大最小公平算法提供了许多理想的属性，例如帕累托效率、策略证明性和公平性。本文基于这样的观察：最大-最小公平性在一个强有力的假设下保证了这些属性——用户需求随着时间的推移是静态的——并且，对于动态用户需求的实际情况，最大-最小公平性失去了一个或多个这些属性。我们提出了 Karma，这是针对动态用户需求的最大最小公平性的概括。 Karma 的关键见解是将“记忆”引入最大最小公平性中——在分配资源时，Karma 会考虑用户过去的分配情况：在每个量程中，用户捐赠其未使用的资源，并在其他用户借用这些资源时获得积分； Karma 精心协调用户之间的信用交换（根据用户的即时需求、捐赠资源和借用资源），并根据用户的信用进行优先资源分配。我们从理论上证明，Karma 保证了帕累托效率、在线策略验证性以及动态用户需求的最优公平性（无需未来了解用户需求）。对生产工作负载的实证评估表明，这些属性可以很好地转化为实践：Karma 能够将用户之间的性能差异减少到最低限度，同时保持帕累托最优的系统范围性能。

items.title: MGG: Accelerating Graph Neural Networks with Fine-Grained Intra-Kernel Communication-Computation Pipelining on Multi-GPU Platforms.
items.abstract: The increasing size of input graphs for graph neural networks (GNNs) highlights the demand for using multi-GPU platforms. However, existing multi-GPU GNN systems optimize the computation and communication individually based on the conventional practice of scaling dense DNNs. For irregularly sparse and fine-grained GNN workloads, such solutions miss the opportunity to jointly schedule/optimize the computation and communication operations for high-performance delivery. To this end, we propose MGG , a novel system design to accelerate full-graph GNNs on multi-GPU platforms. The core of MGG is its novel fine-grained dynamic software pipeline to facilitate fine-grained computation-communication overlapping within a GPU kernel. Specifically, MGG introduces GNN-tailored pipeline construction and GPU-aware pipeline mapping to facilitate workload balancing and operation overlapping. MGG also incorporates an intelligent runtime design with analytical modeling and optimization heuristics to dynamically improve the GNN execution performance. Extensive evaluation reveals that MGG outperforms state-of-the-art full-graph GNN systems across various settings: on average 4.41×, 4.81×, and 10.83× faster than DGL, MGG-UVM, and ROC frameworks, respectively.
items.abstract_zh: 图神经网络 (GNN) 输入图尺寸的不断增加凸显了使用多 GPU 平台的需求。然而，现有的多 GPU GNN 系统基于缩放密集 DNN 的传统做法分别优化了计算和通信。对于不规则稀疏和细粒度的 GNN 工作负载，此类解决方案错过了联合调度/优化计算和通信操作以实现高性能交付的机会。为此，我们提出了 MGG，这是一种新颖的系统设计，可在多 GPU 平台上加速全图 GNN。 MGG 的核心是其新颖的细粒度动态软件管道，以促进 GPU 内核内的细粒度计算通信重叠。具体来说，MGG 引入了 GNN 定制的管道构建和 GPU 感知的管道映射，以促进工作负载平衡和操作重叠。 MGG 还将智能运行时设计与分析建模和优化启发法相结合，以动态提高 GNN 执行性能。广泛的评估表明，MGG 在各种设置下都优于最先进的全图 GNN 系统：平均分别比 DGL、MGG-UVM 和 ROC 框架快 4.41 倍、4.81 倍和 10.83 倍。

items.title: SEPH: Scalable, Efficient, and Predictable Hashing on Persistent Memory.
items.abstract: With the merits of high density, non-volatility, and DRAM-scale latency/bandwidth, persistent memory (PM) brings hope to high-performance storage systems, in which hashing-based index structures receive great attention owing to the efficient query performance. Though lots of efforts have been made to rethink the hashing schemes for PM in recent years, nevertheless, based on our investigation, none of them can hit performance scalability, efficiency, and predictability with one stone, seriously limiting their practicality to time-sensitive or latency-critical applications. To this end, this paper presents SEPH, a Scalable, Efficient, and Predictable Hashing for PM. SEPH paves a new direction to build the hash table by introducing the novel Level Segment (LS) structure, a key to breaking the dilemma between efficiency and predictability standing in front of the existing hashing schemes for PM. With the LS-based hash table structure, SEPH further enables a low-overhead split to greatly suppress the resizing-incurred unpredictability, and develops a semi lock-free concurrency control that requires a nearly-minimal amount of writes to handle an item insertion for achieving ever-higher efficiency and scalability while ensuring the correctness and crash consistency. Compared to state-of-the-art hashing schemes, SEPH demonstrates higher efficiency (up to 15.4× higher throughput), better scalability (performance scales up to 48 threads), and more reliable predictability (improving the tail latency by up to 19.3×).
items.abstract_zh: 凭借高密度、非易失性和 DRAM 级延迟/带宽的优点，持久内存（PM）为高性能存储系统带来了希望，其中基于哈希的索引结构由于高效的查询性能而受到广泛关注。尽管近年来人们做出了很多努力来重新思考 PM 的哈希方案，但根据我们的调查，它们都无法一劳永逸地实现性能可扩展性、效率和可预测性，严重限制了它们在时间敏感或延迟关键型应用程序中的实用性。为此，本文提出了 SEPH，一种可扩展、高效且可预测的 PM 哈希。 SEPH 通过引入新颖的级别段 (LS) 结构，为构建哈希表开辟了新的方向，这是打破现有 PM 哈希方案面前效率和可预测性之间困境的关键。通过基于LS的哈希表结构，SEPH进一步实现了低开销的分割，极大地抑制了大小调整带来的不可预测性，并开发了半无锁并发控制，需要几乎最小的写入量来处理项目插入，从而在保证正确性和崩溃一致性的同时实现更高的效率和可扩展性。与最先进的哈希方案相比，SEPH 表现出更高的效率（吞吐量提高高达 15.4 倍）、更好的可扩展性（性能可扩展到 48 个线程）和更可靠的可预测性（将尾部延迟提高高达 19.3 倍）。

items.title: BWoS: Formally Verified Block-based Work Stealing for Parallel Processing.
items.abstract: Work stealing is a widely-used scheduling technique for parallel processing on multicore. Each core owns a queue of tasks and avoids idling by stealing tasks from other queues. Prior work mostly focuses on balancing workload among cores, disregarding whether stealing may adversely impact the owner's performance or hinder synchronization optimizations. Real-world industrial runtimes for parallel processing heavily rely on work-stealing queues for scalability, and such queues can become bottlenecks to their performance. We present Block-based Work Stealing (BWoS), a novel and pragmatic design that splits per-core queues into multiple blocks. Thieves and owners rarely operate on the same blocks, greatly removing interferences and enabling aggressive optimizations on the owner's synchronization with thieves. Furthermore, BWoS enables a novel probabilistic stealing policy that guarantees thieves steal from longer queues with higher probability. In our evaluation, using BWoS improves performance by up to 1.25x in the Renaissance macrobenchmark when applied to Java GC, provides average 1.26x speedup in JSON processing when applied to Go runtime, and improves maximum throughput of Hyper HTTP server by 1.12x when applied to Rust Tokio runtime. In microbenchmarks, it provides 8-11x better performance than state-of-the-art designs. We have formally verified and optimized BWoS on weak memory models with a model-checking-based framework.
items.abstract_zh: 工作窃取是一种广泛使用的多核并行处理调度技术。每个核心都拥有一个任务队列，并通过从其他队列窃取任务来避免空闲。先前的工作主要集中在平衡核心之间的工作负载，而忽略窃取是否会对所有者的性能产生不利影响或阻碍同步优化。现实世界中的并行处理工业运行时严重依赖工作窃取队列来实现可扩展性，并且此类队列可能成为其性能的瓶颈。我们提出了基于块的工作窃取（BWoS），这是一种新颖且实用的设计，可将每个核心队列拆分为多个块。盗贼和所有者很少在同一块上进行操作，这极大地消除了干扰，并能够对所有者与盗贼的同步进行积极优化。此外，BWoS 还实现了一种新颖的概率盗窃策略，保证窃贼以更高的概率从更长的队列中进行盗窃。在我们的评估中，当应用于 Java GC 时，使用 BWoS 在 Renaissance 宏基准测试中将性能提高了高达 1.25 倍；当应用于 Go 运行时时，JSON 处理的平均速度提高了 1.26 倍；当应用于 Rust Tokio 运行时时，Hyper HTTP 服务器的最大吞吐量提高了 1.12 倍。在微基准测试中，它的性能比最先进的设计高 8-11 倍。我们使用基于模型检查的框架在弱内存模型上正式验证和优化了 BWoS。

items.title: Characterizing Off-path SmartNIC for Accelerating Distributed Systems.
items.abstract: SmartNICs have recently emerged as an appealing device for accelerating distributed systems. However, there has not been a comprehensive characterization of SmartNICs, and existing designs typically only leverage a single communication path for workload offloading. This paper presents the first holistic study of a representative off-path SmartNIC, specifically the Bluefield-2, from a communication-path perspective. Our experimental study systematically explores the key performance characteristics of communication among the client, on-board SoC, and host, and offers insightful findings and advice for designers. Moreover, we propose the concurrent use of multiple communication paths of a SmartNIC and present a pioneering guideline to expose new optimization opportunities for various distributed systems. To demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach, we conducted case studies on a SmartNIC-based distributed file system (LineFS) and an RDMA-based disaggregated key-value store (DrTM-KV). Our experimental results show improvements of up to 30% and 25% for LineFS and DrTM-KV, respectively.
items.abstract_zh: SmartNIC 最近已成为加速分布式系统的有吸引力的设备。然而，SmartNIC 尚未得到全面的表征，并且现有设计通常仅利用单个通信路径来减轻工作负载。本文从通信路径的角度首次对具有代表性的路径外 SmartNIC（特别是 Bluefield-2）进行全面研究。我们的实验研究系统地探索了客户端、板载 SoC 和主机之间通信的关键性能特征，并为设计人员提供了富有洞察力的发现和建议。此外，我们建议同时使用 SmartNIC 的多个通信路径，并提出开创性指南，为各种分布式系统提供新的优化机会。为了证明我们方法的有效性，我们对基于 SmartNIC 的分布式文件系统 (LineFS) 和基于 RDMA 的分类键值存储 (DrTM-KV) 进行了案例研究。我们的实验结果表明，LineFS 和 DrTM-KV 分别提高了 30% 和 25%。

items.title: No Provisioned Concurrency: Fast RDMA-codesigned Remote Fork for Serverless Computing.
items.abstract: Serverless platforms essentially face a tradeoff between container startup time and provisioned concurrency (i.e., cached instances), which is further exaggerated by the frequent need for remote container initialization. This paper presents MITOSIS, an operating system primitive that provides fast remote fork, which exploits a deep codesign of the OS kernel with RDMA. By leveraging the fast remote read capability of RDMA and partial state transfer across serverless containers, MITOSIS bridges the performance gap between local and remote container initialization. MITOSIS is the first to fork over 10,000 new containers from one instance across multiple machines within a second, while allowing the new containers to efficiently transfer the pre-materialized states of the forked one. We have implemented MITOSIS on Linux and integrated it with FN, a popular serverless platform. Under load spikes in real-world serverless workloads, MITOSIS reduces the function tail latency by 89% with orders of magnitude lower memory usage. For serverless workflow that requires state transfer, MITOSIS improves its execution time by 86%.
items.abstract_zh: 无服务器平台本质上面临容器启动时间和预配置并发（即缓存实例）之间的权衡，而远程容器初始化的频繁需求进一步加剧了这种权衡。本文介绍了 MITOSIS，这是一种提供快速远程分叉的操作系统原语，它利用了操作系统内核与 RDMA 的深度协同设计。通过利用 RDMA 的快速远程读取功能以及跨无服务器容器的部分状态传输，MITOSIS 弥合了本地和远程容器初始化之间的性能差距。 MITOSIS 是第一个在一秒钟内从一个实例跨多台机器分叉出 10,000 多个新容器的公司，同时允许新容器有效地传输分叉容器的预物化状态。我们在 Linux 上实现了 MITOSIS，并将其与流行的无服务器平台 FN 集成。在实际无服务器工作负载的负载峰值下，MITOSIS 将函数尾部延迟减少了 89%，同时内存使用量降低了几个数量级。对于需要状态传输的无服务器工作流程，MITOSIS 将其执行时间提高了 86%。

items.title: Cocktailer: Analyzing and Optimizing Dynamic Control Flow in Deep Learning.
items.abstract: With the growing complexity of deep neural networks (DNNs), developing DNN programs with intricate control flow logic (e.g., loops, branches, and recursion) has become increasingly essential. However, executing such DNN programs efficiently on accelerators is challenging. Current DNN frameworks typically process control flow on the CPU, while offloading the remaining computations to accelerators like GPUs. This often introduces significant synchronization overhead between CPU and the accelerator, and prevents global optimization across control flow scopes. To address this challenge, we propose Cocktailer, a new DNN compiler that co-optimizes the execution of control flow and data flow on hardware accelerators. Cocktailer provides the uTask abstraction to unify the representation of DNN models, including both control flow and data flow. This allows Cocktailer to expose a holistic scheduling space for rescheduling control flow to the lower-level hardware parallelism of accelerators. Cocktailer uses a heuristic policy to find efficient schedules and is able to automatically move control flow into kernels of accelerators, enabling optimization across control flow boundaries. Evaluations demonstrate that Cocktailer can accelerate DNN models with control flow by up to 8.2× over the fastest one of the state-of-the-art DNN frameworks and compilers.
items.abstract_zh: 随着深度神经网络 (DNN) 的复杂性不断增加，开发具有复杂控制流逻辑（例如循环、分支和递归）的 DNN 程序变得越来越重要。然而，在加速器上高效执行此类 DNN 程序具有挑战性。当前的 DNN 框架通常在 CPU 上处理控制流，同时将剩余的计算卸载到 GPU 等加速器。这通常会在 CPU 和加速器之间引入显着的同步开销，并阻止跨控制流范围的全局优化。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了 Cocktailer，这是一种新的 DNN 编译器，可以共同优化硬件加速器上控制流和数据流的执行。 Cocktailer 提供了 uTask 抽象来统一 DNN 模型的表示，包括控制流和数据流。这使得 Cocktailer 能够公开一个整体调度空间，用于将控制流重新调度到加速器的较低级别硬件并行性。 Cocktailer 使用启发式策略来找到有效的调度，并能够自动将控制流移至加速器的内核中，从而实现跨控制流边界的优化。评估表明，与最快的最先进的 DNN 框架和编译器之一相比，Cocktailer 的控制流可将 DNN 模型加速高达 8.2 倍。

items.title: VBASE: Unifying Online Vector Similarity Search and Relational Queries via Relaxed Monotonicity.
items.abstract: Approximate similarity queries on high-dimensional vector indices have become the cornerstone for many critical online services. An increasing need for more sophisticated vector queries requires integrating vector search systems with relational databases. However, high-dimensional vector indices do not exhibit monotonicity, a critical property of conventional indices. The lack of monotonicity forces existing vector systems to rely on monotonicity-preserving tentative indices, set up temporarily for a target vector's TopK nearest neighbors, to facilitate queries. This leads to suboptimal performance due to the difficulty to predict the optimal K. This paper presents VBase, a system that efficiently supports complex queries of both approximate similarity search and relational operators. VBase identifies a common property, relaxed monotonicity, to unify two seemingly incompatible systems. This common property allows VBase to circumvent the constraints of a TopK-only interface to achieve significantly higher efficiency, while provably preserving the semantics of TopK-based solutions. Evaluation results show VBase offers up to three orders-of-magnitude higher performance than state-of-the-art vector systems on complex online vector queries. VBase further enables analytical similarity queries that previous vector systems do not, and shows 7,000× speedup with 99.9% accuracy of exact queries.
items.abstract_zh: 高维向量索引的近似相似度查询已成为许多关键在线服务的基石。对更复杂的矢量查询的需求不断增长，需要将矢量搜索系统与关系数据库集成。然而，高维向量索引不表现出单调性，而单调性是传统索引的关键属性。缺乏单调性迫使现有向量系统依赖于为目标向量的 TopK 最近邻居临时设置的保持单调性的暂定索引，以方便查询。由于难以预测最佳 K，这会导致性能不佳。本文提出了 VBase，这是一个有效支持近似相似性搜索和关系运算符的复杂查询的系统。 VBase 确定了一个共同的属性，即宽松的单调性，以统一两个看似不兼容的系统。这一公共属性允许 VBase 绕过仅 TopK 接口的限制，以显着提高效率，同时可证明保留基于 TopK 的解决方案的语义。评估结果显示，在复杂的在线矢量查询方面，VBase 的性能比最先进的矢量系统高出三个数量级。 VBase 进一步实现了以前矢量系统无法实现的分析相似性查询，并显示出 7,000 倍的加速和 99.9% 的精确查询准确率。

items.title: Effectively Scheduling Computational Graphs of Deep Neural Networks toward Their Domain-Specific Accelerators.
items.abstract: Fully exploiting the computing power of an accelerator specialized for deep neural networks (DNNs) calls for the synergy between network and hardware architectures, but existing approaches partition a computational graph of DNN into multiple sub-graphs by abstracting away hardware architecture and assign resources to each sub-graph, not only producing redundant off-core data movements but also under-utilizing the hardware resources of a domain-specific architecture (DSA). This paper introduces a systematic approach for effectively scheduling DNN computational graphs on DSA platforms. By fully taking into account hardware architecture when partitioning a computational graph into coarse-grained sub-graphs, our work enables the synergy between network and hardware architectures, addressing several challenges of prior work: (1) it produces larger but fewer kernels, converting a large number of off-core data movements into on-core data exchanges; (2) it exploits the imbalanced memory usage distribution across DNN network architecture, better saturating the DSA memory hierarchy; (3) it enables across-layer instruction scheduling not studied before, further exploiting the parallelism across different specialized compute units. Results of seven DNN inference models on a DSA platform show that our work outperforms TVM and AStitch by 11.15× and 6.16×, respectively, and obtains throughput competitive to the vendor-crafted implementation. A case study on GPU also demonstrates that generating kernels for our sub-graphs can surpass CUTLASS with and without convolution fusion by 1.06× and 1.23×, respectively.
items.abstract_zh: 充分利用深度神经网络（DNN）专用加速器的计算能力需要网络和硬件架构之间的协同作用，但现有方法通过抽象硬件架构并将资源分配给每个子图，将DNN计算图划分为多个子图，不仅产生冗余的核外数据移动，而且未充分利用特定领域架构（DSA）的硬件资源。本文介绍了一种在 DSA 平台上有效调度 DNN 计算图的系统方法。通过在将计算图划分为粗粒度子图时充分考虑硬件架构，我们的工作实现了网络和硬件架构之间的协同作用，解决了先前工作的几个挑战：（1）它产生更大但更少的内核，将大量的非核心数据移动转换为核心数据交换； (2)它利用了整个DNN网络架构中不平衡的内存使用分布，更好地饱和了DSA内存层次结构； (3)它实现了以前未研究过的跨层指令调度，进一步利用了不同专用计算单元之间的并行性。 DSA 平台上的七个 DNN 推理模型的结果表明，我们的工作分别比 TVM 和 AStitch 好 11.15 倍和 6.16 倍，并且获得了与供应商设计的实现相竞争的吞吐量。 GPU 上的案例研究还表明，为子图生成内核可以在有和没有卷积融合的情况下分别超过 CUTLASS 1.06 倍和 1.23 倍。

items.title: EINNET: Optimizing Tensor Programs with Derivation-Based Transformations.
items.abstract: Boosting the execution performance of deep neural networks (DNNs) is critical due to their wide adoption in real-world applications. However, existing approaches to optimizing the tensor computation of DNNs only consider transformations representable by a fixed set of predefined tensor operators, resulting in a highly restricted optimization space. To address this issue, we propose EinNet, a derivation-based tensor program optimizer. EinNet optimizes tensor programs by leveraging transformations between general tensor algebra expressions and automatically creating new operators desired by transformations, enabling a significantly larger search space that includes those supported by prior works as special cases. Evaluation on seven DNNs shows that EinNet outperforms existing tensor program optimizers by up to 2.72× (1.52× on average) on NVIDIA A100 and up to 2.68× (1.55× on average) on NVIDIA V100. EinNet is publicly available at https://github.com/InfiniTensor/InfiniTensor .
items.abstract_zh: 由于深度神经网络 (DNN) 在实际应用中得到广泛采用，提高其执行性能至关重要。然而，现有的优化 DNN 张量计算的方法仅考虑由一组固定的预定义张量算子表示的变换，导致优化空间受到高度限制。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 EinNet，一种基于推导的张量程序优化器。 EinNet 通过利用一般张量代数表达式之间的转换并自动创建转换所需的新运算符来优化张量程序，从而实现更大的搜索空间，其中包括先前工作作为特殊情况支持的搜索空间。对七个 DNN 的评估表明，EinNet 在 NVIDIA A100 上的性能优于现有张量程序优化器，最高可达 2.72 倍（平均 1.52 倍），在 NVIDIA V100 上最高可达 2.68 倍（平均 1.55 倍）。 EinNet 已在 https://github.com/InfiniTensor/InfiniTensor 上公开发布。

items.title: Core slicing: closing the gap between leaky confidential VMs and bare-metal cloud.
items.abstract: Virtual machines are the basis of resource isolation in today's public clouds, yet the security risks of entrusting that isolation to a cloud provider's hypervisor are substantial. Such concerns have motivated the design of hardware extensions for "confidential VMs" that seek to remove the hypervisor from the trusted computing base by adding a highly-privileged firmware layer that checks hypervisor actions, and supports memory encryption and remote attestation. However, the hypervisor retains control of resource management and observes associated actions of the guest including nested page table faults and CPU scheduling, and thus confidential VMs remain vulnerable to an ever-changing variety of hypervisor-level side channel attacks. Bare-metal cloud servers avoid such leaks, but remain a niche due to the high cost of dedicated hardware. We observe that typical cloud VMs run with a static allocation of memory and discrete cores, and increasingly rely on I/O offload, thus negating the apparent need for a hypervisor and the fragile hypervisor/guest isolation boundary. Our design, core slicing , enables multiple untrusted guest OSes to run on shared bare-metal hardware. To ensure isolation without the complexity of virtualization, we propose simple hardware extensions that restrict guests to a static slice of a machine's cores, memory and virtual I/O devices, and delegate resource allocation to a dedicated management slice. We demonstrate practicality and evaluate performance with prototypes for RISC-V and x86.
items.abstract_zh: 虚拟机是当今公共云中资源隔离的基础，但将这种隔离委托给云提供商的虚拟机管理程序的安全风险很大。这些担忧促使人们设计了“机密虚拟机”的硬件扩展，通过添加检查虚拟机管理程序操作并支持内存加密和远程认证的高权限固件层，寻求从可信计算基础中删除虚拟机管理程序。然而，虚拟机管理程序保留对资源管理的控制，并观察来宾的相关操作，包括嵌套页表错误和 CPU 调度，因此机密虚拟机仍然容易受到各种不断变化的虚拟机管理程序级侧通道攻击。裸机云服务器避免了此类泄漏，但由于专用硬件的高成本，仍然是一个利基市场。我们观察到，典型的云虚拟机以静态分配的内存和离散内核运行，并且越来越依赖 I/O 卸载，从而消除了对虚拟机管理程序和脆弱的虚拟机管理程序/来宾隔离边界的明显需求。我们的设计，核心切片，使多个不受信任的客户操作系统能够在共享的裸机硬件上运行。为了确保隔离而不存在虚拟化的复杂性，我们提出了简单的硬件扩展，将来宾限制为机器核心、内存和虚拟 I/O 设备的静态切片，并将资源分配委托给专用管理切片。我们通过 RISC-V 和 x86 原型展示实用性并评估性能。

items.title: ExoFlow: A Universal Workflow System for Exactly-Once DAGs.
items.abstract: Given the fundamental tradeoff between run-time and recovery performance, current distributed systems often build application-specific recovery strategies to minimize overheads. However, it is increasingly common for different applications to be composed into heterogeneous pipelines. Implementing multiple interoperable recovery techniques in the same system is rare and difficult. Thus, today's users must choose between: (1) building on a single system, and face a fixed choice of performance vs. recovery overheads, or (2) the challenging task of stitching together multiple systems that can offer application-specific tradeoffs. We present ExoFlow, a universal workflow system that enables a flexible choice of recovery vs. performance tradeoffs, even within the same application. The key insight behind our solution is to decouple execution from recovery and provide exactly-once semantics as a separate layer from execution. For generality, workflow tasks can return references that capture arbitrary inter-task communication. To enable the workflow system and therefore the end user to take control of recovery, we design task annotations that specify execution semantics such as nondeterminism. ExoFlow generalizes recovery for existing workflow applications ranging from ETL pipelines to stateful serverless workflows, while enabling further optimizations in task communication and recovery.
items.abstract_zh: 考虑到运行时和恢复性能之间的基本权衡，当前的分布式系统通常构建特定于应用程序的恢复策略以最小化开销。然而，将不同的应用程序组合成异构管道的情况越来越普遍。在同一个系统中实现多种可互操作的恢复技术是罕见且困难的。因此，今天的用户必须在以下两者之间做出选择：(1) 在单个系统上构建，并面临性能与恢复开销的固定选择，或者 (2) 将多个系统拼接在一起以提供特定于应用程序的权衡的挑战性任务。我们推出了 ExoFlow，这是一种通用工作流程系统，即使在同一应用程序中，也可以灵活选择恢复与性能权衡。我们的解决方案背后的关键见解是将执行与恢复分离，并提供一次性语义作为与执行分开的层。出于一般性，工作流任务可以返回捕获任意任务间通信的引用。为了使工作流系统以及最终用户能够控制恢复，我们设计了指定执行语义（例如不确定性）的任务注释。 ExoFlow 概括了从 ETL 管道到有状态无服务器工作流的现有工作流应用程序的恢复，同时实现了任务通信和恢复的进一步优化。
